title: Hardware Asset Coverage — Agents Missing on In-Scope Servers

compliance.assurance_rationale: CSF 2.0 ID.AM-01 (Asset inventory) — UC identifies in-scope servers missing forwarder agents by correlating CMDB with Splunk metadata, providing partial evidence of hardware asset inventory completeness.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF ID.AM-01 (Asset inventory) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.16: Hardware Asset Coverage — Agents Missing on In-Scope Servers.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.16' running on _internal metrics; cmdb_servers.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| metadata type=hosts index=*
| lookup cmdb_servers.csv host OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true"
| join type=left host [| search index=_internal source=*metrics.log* group=tcpin_connections earliest=-24h | stats dc(sourceHost) as reporting by sourceHost | rename sourceHost as host]
| where isnull(reporting)
description: Shows servers in CMDB without forwarder heartbeat so asset management inventories are complete for CSF Identify.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes _internal. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Align host naming; (2) weekly diff to CMDB owners; (3) track closure in ID.AM improvement backlog. Indexes required: `index=_internal`; Lookups: `cmdb_servers`, `cmdb_servers.csv`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for shows servers in cmdb without forwarder heartbeat so asset management inventories are complete for csf identify. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Universal Forwarder `_internal`, CMDB lookup.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `_internal` metrics; `cmdb_servers.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align host naming; (2) weekly diff to CMDB owners; (3) track closure in ID.AM improvement backlog.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| metadata type=hosts index=*
| lookup cmdb_servers.csv host OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true"
| join type=left host [| search index=_internal source=*metrics.log* group=tcpin_connections earliest=-24h | stats dc(sourceHost) as reporting by sourceHost | rename sourceHost as host]
| where isnull(reporting)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Hardware Asset Coverage — Agents Missing on In-Scope Servers (ID.AM-01)** — Shows servers in CMDB without forwarder heartbeat so asset management inventories are complete for CSF Identify.

Documented **Data sources**: `_internal` metrics; `cmdb_servers.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Universal Forwarder `_internal`, CMDB lookup. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: *.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `metadata`/`metasearch` to inspect indexes, sources, hosts, or sourcetypes (not raw events).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where in_scope="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(reporting)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: host, reporting, sourceHost.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value
